title: Fatty Tender Turkish Corn Flour Lahmacun
description: A rich and savory Turkish dish featuring corn flour, miso-marinate, and a crispy crust. Serve with a dollop of yogurt and a sprinkle of sumac.
instructions: Combine corn flour and warm water in a bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a small bowl, mix together miso paste, olive oil, onion, minced lamb, crushed tomatoes, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Spread a quarter of the lamb mixture onto each dough circle, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Heat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Cook the lahmacun for 2-3 minutes on each side, until the crust is golden brown and the filling is cooked through.
Serve hot with a dollop of yogurt and a sprinkle of sumac.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
